form_analysis.form_header_text: Rep. Ayanna Pressley (MA-07) FY 27 Appropriations Submission Form
form_analysis.form_instructions_text:
Thank you for your interest in the federal appropriations process. You may submit a Fiscal Year (FY) 2027 appropriations request by using this form. Please submit one form per request and complete each form in its entirety. If you have any questions, please do not hesitate to call our Washington, DC office at (202) 225-5111.

The following sections are included and must be completed pending the type of request:

Section 1: Contact Information 
Section 2: Summary of Request
Section 3: Program Request
Section 4: Report Language Request
Section 5: Bill Report Language Request
Section 6: Grant Information
Section 7: Acknowledgement

fields.label: Requesting Organization/Entity
City
Zip Code
First Name
Last Name
Email Address
Phone Number
Appropriations Subcommittee
options: Agriculture, Rural Development, Food and Drug Administration, and Related Agencies
Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies
Energy and Water Development, and Related Agencies
Labor, Health and Human Services, Education, and Related Agencies
Military Construction, Veterans Affairs, and Related Agencies
State, Foreign Operations, and Related Programs
Transportation, and Housing and Urban Development, and Related Agencies
fields.draft_value: Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies
fields.rationale: NASA funding falls under the jurisdiction of the CJS subcommittee.

fields.label: Please select the type of request
fields.rationale: The request is for a specific funding level for an existing NASA directorate ($9 billion), which is a programmatic request.

fields.label: Agency
fields.rationale: Directly from reference values.

fields.label: Account/Program Name
fields.rationale: Standardized program name from SMD topline funding context.

fields.label: Priority Level
fields.rationale: Priority ranking is specific to the submitter's internal strategy and is not provided in the context.

fields.label: If "Programmatic Funding Request", please select one of the options below:
options: Support total funding level of: $ (fill in below)
Support the President's FY 2027 budget request
fields.draft_value: Support total funding level of: $ (fill in below)
fields.rationale: The request specifies a target amount of $9 billion, which is an increase over the FY2026 enacted level and the known budget requests.

fields.label: Total Amount of Program Request for FY 2027
fields.rationale: The explicit funding ask provided in the reference values.

fields.label: Total Amount of FY 2026 (if applicable):
fields.rationale: Based on the pre-filled value for the FY2026 enacted amount.

fields.label: FY 27 President's Budget Request (if available):
fields.rationale: The FY2027 President's Budget Request has not been released yet per the reference context.

fields.label: Why is the request a good use of taxpayer dollars? Please fully state your justification for this request:
fields.draft_value:
* Every $1 invested in NASA returns approximately $3 to the economy, driving industrial and scientific innovation across all 50 states
* Supports the peer-reviewed Decadal Survey priorities, providing a nonpartisan, high-ROI roadmap for transformative discoveries
* Ensures U.S. leadership in space science to set exploration norms in the face of rapid competition from China's expanding lunar and planetary programs
* Sustains a critical STEM talent pipeline and aerospace workforce that is difficult to rebuild once lost to layoffs or budget instability
fields.rationale: Uses Advocacy Talking Points regarding economic ROI, international competition, and workforce stability.

fields.label: Please explain the request's importance to Massachusetts 7th Congressional District.
fields.draft_value:
* NASA Science obligated over $140.4M to MA-07 institutions from FY23-FY25, including $115M to MIT and $14M to Boston University
* Supports management of major missions like the Transiting Exoplanet Survey Satellite (TESS) and the AGAGE global atmospheric gas network at MIT
* Funds critical research at Boston University on solar wind radiation and 3D biomedical tissue modeling at Brigham & Womens Hospital
* Drives local innovation through $11.5M in educational grants to WGBH and advanced flight system development at Northeastern University
fields.rationale: Cites specific spending data for MA-07, including the $140M total and project-specific details for MIT, BU, and other local recipients.

fields.label: List all organizations supporting your request.
fields.draft_value:
* American Astronomical Society (AAS)
* American Geophysical Union (AGU)
fields.rationale: Lists allied organizations from the provided reference values.

fields.label: Please list the names of all Members of Congress to which you are submitting this request:
fields.rationale: This requires submitter-specific knowledge regarding which other offices they are targeting.

fields.label: Include the proposed text of the report language you are requesting:
fields.rationale: The request type is programmatic and reference values state report language is N/A.

fields.rationale: The request type is programmatic and reference values state report language is N/A.

fields.rationale: Requires a specific link to a formal request document which is not provided.

fields.rationale: Requires a specific page number from a source document which is not provided.

fields.rationale: The pre-computed reference values for 'Prior Year Enacted' specifically cite FY2026 as the most recent data point for current-year context.

top_contracts.description: Transiting Exoplanet Survey Satellite (TESS) Project.  This contract is for performance of the phase b bridge phase of the TESS mission. Efforts included, but limited to, are as follows:   develop documentation, plans, and schedules   develop requirements in preparation for the Systems requirement review   perform the technical trade studies outlined in the concept study report, and any other trades identified during phase b bridge phase   provide overall management for the payload development   provide TESS Systems engineering support to the Project in coordination with the GSFC Project Systems Engineer (PSE)   manage the contracts of vendors and subcontractors as necessary during the phase b bridge phase   establish the electronic database of TESS technical requirements and specifications as each document is baselined.  Maintain complete traceability and configuration control of all TESS Project requirement documents.   Deliver documentation in accordance with contract requirements.
top_contracts.generated_award_summary: Managing development for the Transiting Exoplanet Survey Satellite (TESS)

top_contracts.description: The purpose of this contract is to continue the development and maintenance of NASA'S Very Long Baseline Interferometry (VLBI) stations.
top_contracts.generated_award_summary: Maintaining NASA's VLBI network for accurate space measurements
Building complex 3D tissue models for biomedical testing.

top_contracts.description: Bioengineer long-lasting 3d neurovascular microphysiological system to model chronic inflammation-mediated neurodegeneration
top_contracts.generated_award_summary: Developing 3D systems to model chronic inflammation-related neurodegeneration.

top_contracts.description: Advanced x-ray imaging satellite (AXIS) phase a research
top_contracts.generated_award_summary: Feasibility study for the Advanced X-ray Imaging Satellite (AXIS)

top_grants.generated_award_summary: Developing a prototype flight system for small-scale technology demonstration.
